Let me trace through both sequences more carefully to identify where the inserts go and what the BsaI recognition sites should be. Let me trace through the exact boundaries to confirm where the original gene ends and the new fusion begins. I need to pinpoint exactly where the input sequence and output sequence diverge, and then where they reconverge after the insertions are made. I'm tracing through where the sequences diverge and reconverge. The output starts with a shared `atg` codon from the junction, then switches to EGFP (minus its start codon), and I need to find where it transitions back to the original sequence by checking the terminal regions of each component. I'm seeing that snap's stop codon `taa` at the end overlaps with the beginning of the backbone sequence `tgaggatccc...` in the output, so that `taa` serves double duty as both the terminator and the start of the next segment. the `taa` stop codon from snap's end aligns perfectly with the original gene's stop codon in the input, so both sequences transition identically from the stop codon into the backbone sequence `tgaggatccc`.\n\nNow I'm thinking through the Golden Gate assembly mechanics—BsaI-HF v2 recognizes GGTCTC and creates 4-base overhangs that enable directional assembly of the four fragments (vector backbone, egfp, flag, and snap) in the correct order.\n\nSince the plasmid is circular, I need to identify where to linearize it and trace through each junction to verify the overhangs align properly. Looking at the egfp-flag junction in the output, egfp ends with `ctgtacaa` and flag begins with `ggtagtggctcc`, so I'm checking whether these sequences create compatible sticky ends after BsaI digestion. So the junction sequence is `...ctgtacaaaggtagtggctcc...` - egfp(no stop) + flag(no atg). Now I'm checking the flag to snap junction: flag ends without a stop codon and snap begins without the start codon, creating `...ggttca|gacaaagactgc...`. For the final junction between snap and the vector, snap terminates with `...ctgggttaa` and the vector sequence continues from there.\n\nNow I need to figure out the 4-nucleotide overhangs for the Golden Gate assembly at each junction point. Let me trace through where these overhangs actually come from in the output sequence at each fusion site. I need to pinpoint exactly where the BsaI cuts create the 4-base overhangs at each junction—these are the sticky ends that will anneal the fragments together in the Golden Gate assembly. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_013AZiWf8c8Hm6QbReMnh4b7","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GCd6XMYW6tfteoYAtiVGZf","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/design_primers.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Design Golden Gate assembly primers for BsaI-HF v2.\"\"\"\n\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\n\ndef rc(seq):\n    \"\"\"Reverse complement.\"\"\"\n    comp = {'a':'t','t':'a','g':'c','c':'g','A':'T','T':'A','G':'C','C':'G'}\n    return ''.join(comp[b] for b in reversed(seq))\n\ndef calc_tm(seq):\n    \"\"\"Calculate Tm using oligotm with specified parameters.\"\"\"\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        ['oligotm', '-tp', '1', '-sc', '1', '-mv', '50', '-dv', '2', '-n', '0.8', '-d', '500', seq],\n        capture_output=True, text=True\n    )\n    return float(result.stdout.strip())\n\n# Read sequences\nseqs = {}\nwith open('/app/sequences.fasta') as f:\n    name = None\n    for line in f:\n        line = line.strip()\n        if line.startswith('>'):\n            name = line[1:]\n            seqs[name] = ''\n        elif name:\n            seqs[name] += line\n\ninput_seq = seqs['input']\negfp = seqs['egfp']\nflag = seqs['flag']\nsnap = seqs['snap']\noutput_seq = seqs['output']\n\nprint(f\"Input length: {len(input_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"EGFP length: {len(egfp)}\")\nprint(f\"FLAG length: {len(flag)}\")\nprint(f\"SNAP length: {len(snap)}\")\nprint(f\"Output length: {len(output_seq)}\")\n\n# Find where the inserts are in the output\n# EGFP without start codon (atg)\negfp_body = egfp[3:]  # skip ATG\n# EGFP without start and stop\negfp_no_stop = egfp[3:-3]  # skip ATG and TAA\n\n# FLAG without start and stop\nflag_body = flag[3:-3]  # skip ATG and TAA\n\n# SNAP without start, keep stop\nsnap_body = snap[3:]  # skip ATG, keep TAA stop\n\n# Find these in output\negfp_body_start = output_seq.find(egfp[3:-3])  # egfp without atg and without stop\nprint(f\"\\nEGFP body (no ATG, no stop) found at output position: {egfp_body_start}\")\nprint(f\"  EGFP body length (no atg, no stop): {len(egfp[3:-3])}\")\n\nflag_body_start = output_seq.find(flag[3:-3])  # flag without atg and without stop\nprint(f\"FLAG body (no ATG, no stop) found at output position: {flag_body_start}\")\nprint(f\"  FLAG body length (no atg, no stop): {len(flag[3:-3])}\")\n\nsnap_body_start = output_seq.find(snap[3:])  # snap without atg (keep stop)\nprint(f\"SNAP body (no ATG, keep stop) found at output position: {snap_body_start}\")\nprint(f\"  SNAP body length (no atg): {len(snap[3:])}\")\n\n# Let me trace the exact junctions in the output\n# The output is: vector_left + egfp(no stop) + flag(no atg, no stop) + snap(no atg, keep stop) + vector_right\n#\n# But wait - does egfp keep its ATG or not? Let me check more carefully\negfp_full_start = output_seq.find(egfp[:-3])  # egfp with ATG but no stop\nif egfp_full_start >= 0:\n    print(f\"\\nEGFP with ATG (no stop) found at output position: {egfp_full_start}\")\nelse:\n    # Maybe the ATG is shared with catatg\n    egfp_without_atg_start = output_seq.find(egfp[3:-3])\n    print(f\"\\nEGFP without ATG found at: {egfp_without_atg_start}\")\n    # Check what's before it in the output\n    print(f\"  Context before: ...{output_seq[egfp_without_atg_start-10:egfp_without_atg_start]}\")\n    print(f\"  Context after start: {output_seq[egfp_without_atg_start:egfp_without_atg_start+20]}...\")\n\n# Let me find exact junction positions\n# First, find where egfp content starts in output (including its ATG)\negfp_with_atg = egfp[:-3]  # keep ATG, remove stop TAA\npos = output_seq.find(egfp_with_atg)\nif pos >= 0:\n    print(f\"\\nEGFP (with ATG, no stop) starts at output pos {pos}\")\n    egfp_start_in_output = pos\n    egfp_end_in_output = pos + len(egfp_with_atg)\nelse:\n    print(\"\\nEGFP with ATG not found as a whole. Checking overlap with vector...\")\n    # The ATG of egfp may be the same as the ATG in the vector's catatg\n    # Let's check: find where egfp[1:] or egfp[2:] or egfp[3:] starts\n    for skip in range(4):\n        p = output_seq.find(egfp[skip:-3])\n        if p >= 0:\n            print(f\"  egfp[{skip}:-3] found at pos {p}, context before: '{output_seq[max(0,p-6):p]}'\")\n            egfp_start_in_output = p - skip  # account for shared bases\n            egfp_end_in_output = p + len(egfp[skip:-3])\n            break\n\n# Find flag body in output\nflag_no_atg_no_stop = flag[3:-3]\nflag_pos = output_seq.find(flag_no_atg_no_stop)\nprint(f\"\\nFLAG body (no ATG, no stop) at output pos {flag_pos}\")\nflag_start_in_output = flag_pos\nflag_end_in_output = flag_pos + len(flag_no_atg_no_stop)\n\n# Find snap body in output\nsnap_no_atg = snap[3:]  # keep stop\nsnap_pos = output_seq.find(snap_no_atg)\nprint(f\"SNAP body (no ATG, keep stop) at output pos {snap_pos}\")\nsnap_start_in_output = snap_pos\nsnap_end_in_output = snap_pos + len(snap_no_atg)\n\nprint(f\"\\n=== Junction analysis ===\")\nprint(f\"Vector_left ends, EGFP starts at output pos ~{egfp_start_in_output}\")\nprint(f\"EGFP ends, FLAG starts at output pos ~{egfp_end_in_output} / {flag_start_in_output}\")\nprint(f\"FLAG ends, SNAP starts at output pos ~{flag_end_in_output} / {snap_start_in_output}\")\nprint(f\"SNAP ends, Vector_right starts at output pos ~{snap_end_in_output}\")\n\n# The 4-nt overhangs at each junction\n# J1: vector→egfp junction\nj1_region = output_seq[egfp_start_in_output-4:egfp_start_in_output+4]\nprint(f\"\\nJ1 region (vector→egfp): {j1_region}\")\n\n# J2: egfp→flag junction\nj2_region = output_seq[egfp_end_in_output-4:egfp_end_in_output+4]\nprint(f\"J2 region (egfp→flag): {j2_region}\")\n\n# J3: flag→snap junction\nj3_region = output_seq[flag_end_in_output-4:flag_end_in_output+4]\nprint(f\"J3 region (flag→snap): {j3_region}\")\n\n# J4: snap→vector junction\nj4_region = output_seq[snap_end_in_output-4:snap_end_in_output+4]\nprint(f\"J4 region (snap→vector): {j4_region}\")\n\n# Now define the 4-nt overhangs\n# The overhang is the 4 bases at the junction point\n# I need to decide where exactly the cut falls in the junction\n\n# For Golden Gate, the overhangs need to be unique and directional\n# Let me pick 4 bases at each junction from the OUTPUT sequence\n\n# J1 overhang: 4 bases at the vector→egfp boundary\n# This is where egfp starts. Let me use 4 bases starting right at egfp's beginning\nj1_oh = output_seq[egfp_start_in_output:egfp_start_in_output+4]\nprint(f\"\\nJ1 overhang: {j1_oh}\")\n\n# J2 overhang: 4 bases at egfp→flag boundary\nj2_oh = output_seq[flag_start_in_output-4:flag_start_in_output]\n# Or we could straddle: use last 2 of egfp + first 2 of flag\n# Let me use 4 bases right at the junction\nj2_oh = output_seq[egfp_end_in_output-2:egfp_end_in_output+2]\nprint(f\"J2 overhang (straddling egfp-flag): {j2_oh}\")\n\n# Actually, let me think about this more carefully.\n# In Golden Gate, after BsaI cuts, the 4-nt overhang is OUTSIDE the recognition site.\n# The assembled product contains the 4-nt overhang but NOT the BsaI site.\n# So the 4 bases at each junction in the output ARE the overhangs.\n# I need to place the overhangs such that adjacent fragments share them.\n\n# Let me redefine: the output can be split as:\n# [vector_left][OH1][egfp_region][OH2][flag_region][OH3][snap_region][OH4][vector_right]\n# where each OHx is 4 bases\n\n# But actually, the overhangs are part of the fragments - they're at the ends.\n# Fragment 1 (vector): ...vector_left...[OH1]---cut---[OH4]...vector_right...\n# Fragment 2 (egfp): [OH1]...egfp_region...[OH2]\n# Fragment 3 (flag): [OH2]...flag_region...[OH3]\n# Fragment 4 (snap): [OH3]...snap_region...[OH4]\n\n# The overhangs need to be from the output sequence.\n# I need to choose 4 cut points in the output, each yielding a 4-nt overhang.\n\n# Let me reconsider the junctions more carefully.\n# The output has these regions from the input/inserts:\nprint(\"\\n=== Detailed junction analysis ===\")\n\n# Check the exact bases at egfp-flag junction\nprint(f\"End of egfp region in output: ...{output_seq[egfp_end_in_output-10:egfp_end_in_output]}\")\nprint(f\"Start of flag region in output: {output_seq[flag_start_in_output:flag_start_in_output+10]}...\")\nprint(f\"Gap between egfp_end and flag_start: {flag_start_in_output - egfp_end_in_output}\")\n\n# Check the exact bases at flag-snap junction\nprint(f\"End of flag region in output: ...{output_seq[flag_end_in_output-10:flag_end_in_output]}\")\nprint(f\"Start of snap region in output: {output_seq[snap_start_in_output:snap_start_in_output+10]}...\")\nprint(f\"Gap between flag_end and snap_start: {snap_start_in_output - flag_end_in_output}\")\n\n# Check the exact vector junction at the end of snap\nprint(f\"End of snap in output: ...{output_seq[snap_end_in_output-10:snap_end_in_output]}\")\nprint(f\"After snap in output: {output_seq[snap_end_in_output:snap_end_in_output+10]}...\")\n\n# Find where input (vector) sequence diverges from output\n# Both start the same, find where they differ\nfor i in range(min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq))):\n    if input_seq[i] != output_seq[i]:\n        print(f\"\\nInput and output diverge at position {i}\")\n        print(f\"  Input context: ...{input_seq[i-10:i]}|{input_seq[i:i+10]}...\")\n        print(f\"  Output context: ...{output_seq[i-10:i]}|{output_seq[i:i+10]}...\")\n        diverge_pos = i\n        break\n\n# Find where they reconverge (search from end)\n# Since input is circular, the end should match\nfor i in range(1, min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq))):\n    i_pos = len(input_seq) - i\n    o_pos = len(output_seq) - i\n    if input_seq[i_pos] != output_seq[o_pos]:\n        print(f\"\\nInput and output reconverge at {i} bases from end\")\n        print(f\"  Input end context: ...{input_seq[i_pos-5:i_pos+5]}... (pos {i_pos})\")\n        print(f\"  Output end context: ...{output_seq[o_pos-5:o_pos+5]}... (pos {o_pos})\")\n        reconverge_from_end = i - 1\n        break\n\n# The vector contributes: output[0:diverge_pos] and output[-(reconverge_from_end):]\nprint(f\"\\nVector left part: output[0:{diverge_pos}] = ...{output_seq[diverge_pos-5:diverge_pos]}\")\nprint(f\"Vector right part: output[{len(output_seq)-reconverge_from_end}:] = {output_seq[len(output_seq)-reconverge_from_end:len(output_seq)-reconverge_from_end+10]}...\")\nprint(f\"Insert region in output: output[{diverge_pos}:{len(output_seq)-reconverge_from_end}]\")\n\ninsert_region = output_seq[diverge_pos:len(output_seq)-reconverge_from_end]\nprint(f\"Insert region length: {len(insert_region)}\")\nprint(f\"Insert region start: {insert_region[:30]}...\")\nprint(f\"Insert region end: ...{insert_region[-30:]}\")\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":229,"cache_read_input_tokens":41940,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":229,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"56016a6b-2d20-4551-96ed-da3483a614ac","uuid":"91532c89-d69d-4057-99c9-c34e6c635f40"}
